tree.name: Jaylen Brown
tree.note: 2024 NBA Finals MVP, 3x All-Star. Drafted from California.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2016 1st Round Pick (#3) via BKN
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: BKN traded Kevin Garnett, Paul Pierce, Jason Terry, D.J. White, 2017 1st (#27, Kyle Kuzma via pick swap), and 2017 2nd (Sasha Vezenkov) to BOS for Keith Bogans, MarShon Brooks, Kris Humphries, Kris Joseph, Gerald Wallace, 2014 1st (James Young), 2016 1st (#3 Jaylen Brown), 2017 1st (#1 Markelle Fultz, swapped to #3 Jayson Tatum), and 2018 1st (Collin Sexton).
assetsGivenUp.note: One of the most lopsided trades in NBA history. Pick conveyed as #3 in 2016.

assetsGivenUp.name: Kevin Garnett
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Celtics acquire KG for Al Jefferson, Ryan Gomes, Gerald Green, Sebastian Telfair, Theo Ratliff, 2009 1st

assetsGivenUp.name: Al Jefferson
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Drafted by Celtics with their OWN pick

assetsGivenUp.name: Gerald Green
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Drafted by Celtics with their OWN pick
assetsGivenUp.note: High school to NBA, dunk contest winner. TRUE ORIGIN.

assetsGivenUp.name: Ryan Gomes
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Drafted by Celtics with their OWN 2nd round pick

assetsGivenUp.name: Sebastian Telfair
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Celtics trade Randy Foye (#7 pick), Raef LaFrentz, and Dan Dickau to Portland for Sebastian Telfair, Theo Ratliff, and 2008 2nd (#46 Trent Plaisted)

assetsGivenUp.name: Raef LaFrentz
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Acquired from Dallas for Antoine Walker and Tony Delk

assetsGivenUp.name: Antoine Walker
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Drafted by Celtics with their OWN pick
assetsGivenUp.note: 3x All-Star, Celtics finished 33-49 in 1995-96. TRUE ORIGIN.

assetsGivenUp.name: Tony Delk
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Acquired from Phoenix for Rodney Rogers

assetsGivenUp.name: Rodney Rogers
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Signed as unrestricted free agent from LAC
assetsGivenUp.note: Free agent signing - no assets given up. TRUE ORIGIN.

assetsGivenUp.name: Randy Foye
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Drafted #7 overall by Celtics, immediately traded to Portland on draft night
assetsGivenUp.note: BOS own pick. Selected and shipped same day in draft-night deal.

assetsGivenUp.name: Dan Dickau
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Celtics trade 2006 2nd round pick (became Edin Bavcic) to New Orleans for Dan Dickau
assetsGivenUp.note: Journeyman guard, salary filler in the Portland draft-night deal

assetsGivenUp.name: 2006 2nd Round Pick (#56)
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Celtics own 2nd round pick, sent to New Orleans/Oklahoma City Hornets for Dickau
assetsGivenUp.note: Pick acquired from MIA in Antoine Walker 5-team trade (Aug 2005), then sent to NOH

assetsGivenUp.name: Theo Ratliff
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Celtics trade Randy Foye (#7 pick), Raef LaFrentz, and Dan Dickau to Portland for Sebastian Telfair, Theo Ratliff, and 2008 2nd (#46 Trent Plaisted)
assetsGivenUp.note: Draft day trade. Veteran center, expiring contract. Full trade chain in Tatum tree.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2009 1st Round Pick (#11)
assetsGivenUp.note: Pick conveyed #11 to Minnesota, used on Jonny Flynn

assetsGivenUp.name: Paul Pierce
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Drafted by Celtics with their OWN pick
assetsGivenUp.note: The Truth - 2008 Finals MVP, 10x All-Star. Celtics finished 36-46 in 1997-98. TRUE ORIGIN.

assetsGivenUp.name: Jason Terry
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Signed as unrestricted free agent from DAL
assetsGivenUp.note: The Jet - 2011 NBA Champion with Dallas. Signed 3yr/$15M deal. TRUE ORIGIN.

assetsGivenUp.name: D.J. White
assetsGivenUp.note: Forward, signed 10-day contract with BOS (Feb 28, 2013). Previously played in China. Sent to BKN in the KG/Pierce mega-trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2017 1st Round Pick (#27)
assetsGivenUp.note: BOS own 2017 1st, sent to BKN in KG/Pierce trade. BOS got #1 overall from BKN via pick swap, BKN used the swap to get #27. Lakers drafted Kyle Kuzma at #27.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2017 2nd Round Pick
assetsGivenUp.note: Sent to BKN in KG/Pierce trade. Used to draft Sasha Vezenkov.
